methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $232M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Lunar Energy, a Series D climate and energy company founded by Kunal Girotra, has raised $232M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage, including companies like Redwood Materials, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $232M total raised, implying a valuation of $348M. The peer-median method references four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.5B and a range of $700M to $6.0B, directly implying $1.5B.

The triangulated valuation range is $296M to $1.7B, with a median of $924M across both methods. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in the peer set: Redwood Materials trades at $6.0B while earlier-stage peers cluster between $700M and $2.0B. Key uncertainties include Lunar Energy's relative technology maturity, capital efficiency, and addressable market position within the cohort. The stage-progression floor ($348M) reflects the capital invested; the peer median ($1.5B) assumes comparable risk-adjusted positioning within the climate-tech landscape.
